heading: Cross references relating to tax on individuals
label: 5 Cross references relating to tax on individuals
notes: Amendments2003—Subsec. (b)(1). inserted “, astronauts,” after “Forces”.Pub. L. 108–1212002—Subsec. (b)(1). inserted “and victims of certain terrorist attacks” before “on death”.Pub. L. 107–1341986—Subsec. (a)(4). , amended par. (4) generally, substituting “alternative minimum tax” for “minimum tax for taxpayers other than corporations”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 701(e)(4)(A)Subsec. (b)(2), (3). , struck out par. (2) which read: “For limitation on tax where an individual chooses the benefits of income averaging, see section 1301.” and redesignated former par. (3) as (2).Pub. L. 99–514, § 141(b)(2)1982—Subsec. (a)(4). , formerly § 201(c)(4), substituted “section 55” for “sections 55 and 56”.Pub. L. 97–248, § 201(d)(4)1980—Subsec. (a)(4). substituted “sections 55 and 56” for “section 55”.Pub. L. 96–2221978—Subsec. (a)(3). , redesignated par. (4) as (3). Former par. (3), relating to the alternative tax in the case of capital gains, was struck out.Pub. L. 95–600, § 401(b)(2)Subsec. (a)(4), (5). , §§ 401(b)(2), 421(e)(1), redesignated par. (5) as (4) and substituted “taxpayers other than corporations, see section 55” for “preferences, see section 56”. Former par. (4) redesignated (3).Pub. L. 95–6001976—Subsec. (b). redesignated pars. (2), (3), and (4), as (1), (2), (3), respectively, and struck out former par. (1) which referred to section 632 for limitation on tax attributable to sales of oil or gas properties and par. (5) which referred to section 1347 for limitation on tax attributable to claims against the U.S. involving acquisition of property.Pub. L. 94–4551969—Subsec. (a)(5). , added par. (5).Pub. L. 91–172, § 301(b)(2)Subsec. (b). , substituted “tax” for “surtax” in pars. (1) and (5).Pub. L. 91–172, § 803(d)(6)1964—Subsec. (b). redesignated pars. (2), (3), (4), (7) and (8) as pars. (1) to (5), respectively, substituted “where an individual chooses the benefits of income averaging” for “with respect to compensation for longterm services” in par. (3), and struck out former pars. (1), (5) and (6) which referred to tax attributable to receipt of lump sum under annuity, endowment, or life insurance contract, to income from artistic work or inventions, and to back pay, respectively.Pub. L. 88–272

L. 97–248, title II, § 201(e)(1)96 Stat. 421Sept. 3, 1982“The amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 46, 53, 55, 56, 57, 58, 173, 174, 511, 616, 617, 897, 901, 936, 1016, 6015, 6362, 6654, and 7701 of this title] shall apply to taxable years beginning after .”December 31, 1982

Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by applicable to taxable years beginning after , see , set out as a note under .section 401(b)(2) of Pub. L. 95–600section 401(c) of Pub. L. 95–600section 3 of this titleDec. 31, 1978, , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–600, title IV, § 421(g)92 Stat. 2877Nov. 6, 1978“The amendments made by this section [enacting and amending this section and sections 57, 58, 443, 511, 666, 871, 877, 904, 6015, 6362, and 6654 of this title] shall apply to taxable years beginning after , except that the amendment made by paragraph (1) of subsection (b) [amending ] shall apply to sales and exchanges made after , in taxable years ending after such date.”section 55 of this titlesection 57 of this titleDecember 31, 1978July 26, 1978

Effective Date of 1969 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 91–172, title III, § 301(c)83 Stat. 586Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Dec. 30, 1969Oct. 22, 1986“The amendments made by this section [enacting sections 56 to 58 of this title and amending this section and sections 12, 46, 51, 443, 453, 511, 901, 1373, 1375, 6015, and 6654 of this title] shall apply to taxable years ending after . In the case of a taxable year beginning in 1969 and ending in 1970, the tax imposed by section 56 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by subsection (a)) shall be an amount equal to the tax imposed by such section (determined without regard to this sentence) multiplied by a fraction—December 31, 1969“(1)the numerator of which is the number of days in the taxable year occurring after , andDecember 31, 1969“(2)the denominator of which is the number of days in the entire taxable year.”Amendment by applicable to taxable years beginning after , see , set out as a note under .section 803(d)(6) of Pub. L. 91–172section 803(f) of Pub. L. 91–172section 1 of this titleDec. 31, 1970

Effective Date of 1964 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 88–272, title II, § 232(g)78 Stat. 112Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Feb. 26, 1964Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—General ruleExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section [enacting sections 1301 to 1305, amending this section and sections 4, 72, 144, 402, 403, 6511, and omitting former sections 1301 to 1307 of this title] shall apply with respect to taxable years beginning after .December 31, 1963“(2).—Income from an employmentIf, in a taxable year beginning after , an individual or partnership receives or accrues compensation from an employment (as defined by section 1301(b) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] as in effect immediately before the enactment of this Act [] and the employment began before , the tax attributable to such compensation may, at the election of the taxpayer, be computed under the provisions of sections 1301 and 1307 of such Code as in effect immediately before the enactment of this Act. If a taxpayer so elects (at such time and in such manner as the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate by regulations prescribes), he may not choose for such taxable year the benefits provided by part I of subchapter Q of chapter 1 of such Code (relating to income averaging) as amended by this Act and (if he elects to have subsection (e) of such section 1307 apply) section 170(b)(5) of such Code as amended by this Act shall not apply to charitable contributions paid in such taxable year.”December 31, 1963Feb. 26, 1964February 6, 1963

children.heading: Other rates of tax on individuals, etc.
children.label: a Other rates of tax on individuals, etc.

children.text: For rates of tax on nonresident aliens, see section 871.
For doubling of tax on citizens of certain foreign countries, see section 891.
For rate of withholding in the case of nonresident aliens, see section 1441.
For alternative minimum tax, see section 55.
For limitation on tax in case of income of members of Armed Forces, astronauts, and victims of certain terrorist attacks on death, see section 692.
For computation of tax where taxpayer restores substantial amount held under claim of right, see section 1341.
